basic_details.job_title: RITES Assistant Manager Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 24 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: RITES Limited (A Govt. of India Enterprise), Ministry of Railways
basic_details.short_information: Candidates looking for a quick snapshot of RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 can refer to the table below before going through the detailed sections.

application_fee.details: The application fee for RITES Recruitment 2026 must be paid online at the time of application submission and varies by category. | Candidates applying against unreserved posts must pay the full fee at the online application stage, even if they belong to SC/ST/PwBD category. The fee charged from SC/ST/PwBD candidates during online application will be refunded upon their participation in the further selection process (written exam/interview), credited to the original payment source after deducting GST/bank charges, once their category certificate is submitted and scrutinized. Fees paid through any mode other than the one specified will not be accepted, and such applications will be treated as without fee and summarily rejected. Candidates must ensure the invoice is generated after successful payment; if unable to download it, they should immediately email [email protected] with their registration details. | For queries regarding fee payment, candidates can contact Helpdesk No. 011-33557000, Extension 13221, or email [email protected].

qualification.details: Candidates wondering whether they qualify for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 can check the educational qualification, experience, and age limit requirements below. | Candidates must hold a full-time Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering/Technology in Mechanical, Production, Production & Industrial, Manufacturing, Mechanical/Railways, Mechatronics & Automobile, or a combination of the above disciplines in part or whole. | UR/EWS candidates (and SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PWD candidates applying against unreserved posts) need a first-class degree or a minimum of 60% marks. Reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD, as applicable) need a minimum of 50% marks for consideration against reserved posts. The degree must be recognized by AICTE and awarded by a University established under an Act of Parliament/State legislature, or a Deemed University under the UGC Act, 1956. Sections A & B of the Institution of Engineers (India) examination, treated as equivalent to a degree by the Government of India, are also accepted. If the marksheet does not show a class/division or percentage, candidates must submit the conversion formula issued by their university; if no such formula exists, an authorized undertaking is required, and a CGPA of 6 on a 10-point scale will then be treated as 60%. | Candidates need a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience in production or mechanical maintenance or other mechanical engineering areas, gained while working at a project site, factory, or plant. Training (except on-the-job training), internship, teaching, academic work, fellowship, or Ph.D. research experience will not be counted. However, Apprenticeship training under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, in the relevant discipline will be considered towards the minimum experience, subject to fulfilment of other eligibility conditions. | The upper age limit for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 is 32 years as on the last date of application submission (17 August 2026). | For the Mechanical discipline, the post is identified suitable for candidates with locomotor disability categories OA, OL, OAL, BL, Leprosy Cured, and Acid Attack Victims, with functional classifications S, ST, BN, W, SE, MF, C, R, W & RW. Persons with disabilities belonging to an identified category can apply even where no vacancy is specifically reserved for them, and will be considered by the general standard of merit.

salary_details.details: Candidates checking the RITES Assistant Manager salary can note that the pay scale for the post is ₹40,000–1,40,000, with pay fixed at the minimum of the scale for new appointees. | Employees appointed on a regular basis from other PSUs or Government organizations (Central/State/Banks/Autonomous Body), who applied through proper channel or submitted an NOC at the time of interview, are eligible for protection of pay (Basic + DA) as per company policy. In addition to Basic Pay, selected candidates will get DA, fixed/variable allowances, Performance Related Pay, medical facilities, HRA/lease accommodation, and a superannuation package including PF contribution, Gratuity (as per the Gratuity Act), and Post Retirement Medical Scheme. Other benefits include leave as per leave rules, Maternity/Paternity Leave, medical facility, Group Insurance, and Leave Encashment, as applicable to regular employees. | Candidates switching from 8th CPC CDA pay scales to 4th PRC IDA pay scales on appointment/absorption in RITES will be entitled to the benefit of only one fitment, with final fixation done after implementation of the 4th PRC report, if any.

selection_process: S. No. City
1 Delhi/Gurgaon
2 Kolkata
3 Bangalore
4 Mumbai
5 Guwahati
6 Bhubaneswar
Selection for RITES Recruitment 2026 involves three stages - a written test
document scrutiny
and an interview - with a fixed weightage split between the written test and interview.
The written test consists of 125 objective-type questions carrying one mark each

for a duration of 2.5 hours. There is no negative marking; no marks are deducted for incorrect answers. PwBD candidates are eligible for a compensatory time of 50 minutes. A minimum of 50% marks is required for UR/EWS candidates (45% for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD against reserved posts) to be considered further.
Based on the written test result and the number of vacancies
RITES will scrutinize the documents submitted by candidates

and shortlisted candidates will be called for an interview preceded by verification of original documents. It is the candidate's responsibility to upload documents that substantiate all claims made in the application form.
Candidates are called for interview in the ratio of 1:6 to the number of vacancies. Weightage: Written Test – 60%
Interview – 40% (Technical & Professional proficiency: 30
Personality

Communication & Competency: 10). Total: 100%. A minimum of 60% marks in the interview is required for UR/EWS candidates (50% for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PWD against reserved posts) to be placed on the panel. There is no minimum qualifying mark required in the aggregate. Candidates can appear for the selection process in either Hindi or English. Based on RITES's discretion
the written test and/or interview may be conducted at Regional Offices
which will be communicated with the call letters.
Appointment of selected candidates is subject to being found medically fit as per RITES Rules and Standards of Medical Fitness for the post.

Candidates must give two preferences for their choice of written test centre at the time of online application. RITES will make efforts to allot a preferred centre but reserves the right to allot a different one.
All centres except Delhi/Gurgaon are tentative and will depend on the number of candidates and RITES's discretion. The exact address
date
and time will be communicated to candidates separately.

syllabus.details: The written test syllabus for the Mechanical discipline covers core mechanical engineering subjects, similar in scope to the GATE Mechanical syllabus. | Applied Mechanics and Design: Engineering Mechanics, Mechanics of Materials, Theory of Machines, Vibrations, Machine Design. Fluid Mechanics and Thermal Sciences: Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Thermodynamics, Power Engineering, I.C. Engines, Refrigeration & Air-conditioning, Turbomachinery. Materials, Manufacturing and Industrial Engineering: Engineering Materials, Casting/Forming/Joining Processes, Machining & Machine Tool Operations, Metrology & Inspection, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, Production Planning & Control, Inventory Control, Operations Research. Manufacturing Processes: Metal casting, metal forming, metal joining/welding, welding metallurgy, powder metallurgy, non-destructive testing. Testing of Materials: Non-destructive testing (ultrasonic, radiography, magnetic particle, eddy current, dye penetration), physical testing (tensile, hardness, impact, bend, shear, fatigue, creep), chemical testing, metallography. Material Science: Thermal, chemical, electrical, magnetic and mechanical properties of materials, structure of metals, corrosion, heat transfer, air compressors, air conditioning.

how_to_apply: Ensure you satisfy the necessary conditions and requirements before applying.
Apply online through the registration format available in the Career Section of the RITES website, www.rites.com.
Note down the "Registration No." generated on top of the online form for all further communication with RITES.
Carefully fill in the "Identity Proof" details, and ensure the same document is available for production at later stages of selection.
Upload all the required documents under the "Upload Document" section, ensuring scanned copies are clear and visible.
Make the online payment under the "Make Payment" section, and download the final application form after successful payment.
Keep a copy of the submitted application form and carry it at the time of selection, if called.

Applications without successful fee payment will be treated as incomplete and summarily rejected. Candidates can apply for any number of vacancies separately, but only one application per vacancy is allowed, and an application once submitted cannot be altered.

content_sections.heading: RITES Recruitment 2026 Quick Overview
content_sections.text: Candidates looking for a quick snapshot of RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 can refer to the table below before going through the detailed sections.

rows.information: RITES Limited (A Govt. of India Enterprise), Ministry of Railways

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for RITES Assistant Manager Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for RITES Recruitment 2026 should note that the same date range applies to both the online application and the online fee payment. The exact date, time, and venue of the written test and interview will be communicated separately to shortlisted candidates and are not yet announced in the notification.

rows.event: Commencement of Online Application & Fee Payment
Last Date of Online Application & Fee Payment

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: RITES Recruitment 2026 covers a total of 24 Assistant Manager (Mechanical) vacancies, distributed across categories as shown below. One post is reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) on a horizontal basis, and the total includes backlog vacancies. For the PwBD post, RITES will follow this priority order while issuing offers: first, candidates belonging to the disability type detailed in the table above; second, candidates belonging to any disability type that meets the job requirements for the position; and third, if no suitable candidate is found in the first two categories, the vacancy will be filled from any other category.

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering whether they qualify for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 can check the educational qualification, experience, and age limit requirements below. Candidates must hold a full-time Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering/Technology in Mechanical, Production, Production & Industrial, Manufacturing, Mechanical/Railways, Mechatronics & Automobile, or a combination of the above disciplines in part or whole. UR/EWS candidates (and SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PWD candidates applying against unreserved posts) need a first-class degree or a minimum of 60% marks . Reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD, as applicable) need a minimum of 50% marks for consideration against reserved posts. The degree must be recognized by AICTE and awarded by a University established under an Act of Parliament/State legislature, or a Deemed University under the UGC Act, 1956. Sections A & B of the Institution of Engineers (India) examination, treated as equivalent to a degree by the Government of India, are also accepted. If the marksheet does not show a class/division or percentage, candidates must submit the conversion formula issued by their university; if no such formula exists, an authorized undertaking is required, and a CGPA of 6 on a 10-point scale will then be treated as 60%. Candidates need a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience in production or mechanical maintenance or other mechanical engineering areas, gained while working at a project site, factory, or plant. Training (except on-the-job training), internship, teaching, academic work, fellowship, or Ph.D. research experience will not be counted. However, Apprenticeship training under the Apprenticeship Act, 1961, in the relevant discipline will be considered towards the minimum experience, subject to fulfilment of other eligibility conditions. The upper age limit for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026 is 32 years as on the last date of application submission (17 August 2026). For the Mechanical discipline, the post is identified suitable for candidates with locomotor disability categories OA, OL, OAL, BL, Leprosy Cured, and Acid Attack Victims, with functional classifications S, ST, BN, W, SE, MF, C, R, W & RW. Persons with disabilities belonging to an identified category can apply even where no vacancy is specifically reserved for them, and will be considered by the general standard of merit.

rows.age_relaxation: Number of years of service rendered + 3 years
10 years, in addition to relaxation permissible for SC/ST/OBC-NCL, subject to the post being identified suitable for PwBD
5 years (over and above the upper age limit), subject to fulfilling qualification & experience criteria

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection for RITES Recruitment 2026 involves three stages - a written test, document scrutiny, and an interview - with a fixed weightage split between the written test and interview. The written test consists of 125 objective-type questions carrying one mark each, for a duration of 2.5 hours . There is no negative marking ; no marks are deducted for incorrect answers. PwBD candidates are eligible for a compensatory time of 50 minutes . A minimum of 50% marks is required for UR/EWS candidates ( 45% for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD against reserved posts) to be considered further. Based on the written test result and the number of vacancies, RITES will scrutinize the documents submitted by candidates, and shortlisted candidates will be called for an interview preceded by verification of original documents. It is the candidate's responsibility to upload documents that substantiate all claims made in the application form. Candidates are called for interview in the ratio of 1:6 to the number of vacancies. Weightage: Written Test – 60% , Interview – 40% (Technical & Professional proficiency: 30, Personality, Communication & Competency: 10). Total: 100%. A minimum of 60% marks in the interview is required for UR/EWS candidates ( 50% for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PWD against reserved posts) to be placed on the panel. There is no minimum qualifying mark required in the aggregate. Candidates can appear for the selection process in either Hindi or English. Based on RITES's discretion, the written test and/or interview may be conducted at Regional Offices, which will be communicated with the call letters. Appointment of selected candidates is subject to being found medically fit as per RITES Rules and Standards of Medical Fitness for the post. Candidates must give two preferences for their choice of written test centre at the time of online application. RITES will make efforts to allot a preferred centre but reserves the right to allot a different one. All centres except Delhi/Gurgaon are tentative and will depend on the number of candidates and RITES's discretion. The exact address, date, and time will be communicated to candidates separately.

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Written Test Syllabus
content_sections.text: The written test syllabus for the Mechanical discipline covers core mechanical engineering subjects, similar in scope to the GATE Mechanical syllabus. Applied Mechanics and Design: Engineering Mechanics, Mechanics of Materials, Theory of Machines, Vibrations, Machine Design. Fluid Mechanics and Thermal Sciences: Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Thermodynamics, Power Engineering, I.C. Engines, Refrigeration & Air-conditioning, Turbomachinery. Materials, Manufacturing and Industrial Engineering: Engineering Materials, Casting/Forming/Joining Processes, Machining & Machine Tool Operations, Metrology & Inspection, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, Production Planning & Control, Inventory Control, Operations Research. Manufacturing Processes: Metal casting, metal forming, metal joining/welding, welding metallurgy, powder metallurgy, non-destructive testing. Testing of Materials: Non-destructive testing (ultrasonic, radiography, magnetic particle, eddy current, dye penetration), physical testing (tensile, hardness, impact, bend, shear, fatigue, creep), chemical testing, metallography. Material Science: Thermal, chemical, electrical, magnetic and mechanical properties of materials, structure of metals, corrosion, heat transfer, air compressors, air conditioning.

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates checking the RITES Assistant Manager salary can note that the pay scale for the post is ₹40,000–1,40,000 , with pay fixed at the minimum of the scale for new appointees. Employees appointed on a regular basis from other PSUs or Government organizations (Central/State/Banks/Autonomous Body), who applied through proper channel or submitted an NOC at the time of interview, are eligible for protection of pay (Basic + DA) as per company policy. In addition to Basic Pay, selected candidates will get DA, fixed/variable allowances, Performance Related Pay, medical facilities, HRA/lease accommodation, and a superannuation package including PF contribution, Gratuity (as per the Gratuity Act), and Post Retirement Medical Scheme. Other benefits include leave as per leave rules, Maternity/Paternity Leave, medical facility, Group Insurance, and Leave Encashment, as applicable to regular employees. Candidates switching from 8th CPC CDA pay scales to 4th PRC IDA pay scales on appointment/absorption in RITES will be entitled to the benefit of only one fitment, with final fixation done after implementation of the 4th PRC report, if any.

content_sections.heading: RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for RITES Recruitment 2026 must be paid online at the time of application submission and varies by category. Candidates applying against unreserved posts must pay the full fee at the online application stage, even if they belong to SC/ST/PwBD category. The fee charged from SC/ST/PwBD candidates during online application will be refunded upon their participation in the further selection process (written exam/interview), credited to the original payment source after deducting GST/bank charges, once their category certificate is submitted and scrutinized. Fees paid through any mode other than the one specified will not be accepted, and such applications will be treated as without fee and summarily rejected. Candidates must ensure the invoice is generated after successful payment; if unable to download it, they should immediately email [email protected] with their registration details. For queries regarding fee payment, candidates can contact Helpdesk No. 011-33557000, Extension 13221 , or email [email protected] .

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for RITES Assistant Manager Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must upload the following documents during online application, substantiating their eligibility and claims: High School certificate for proof of Date of Birth Certificates and mark statements of all academic/professional qualifications for all semesters/years (10th, 12th, Diploma/Graduation/Post-Graduation as claimed) Percentage Conversion Document issued by the University/Institute (wherever applicable) EWS/SC/ST/OBC Certificate in the prescribed Government of India format (wherever applicable) Proof of Identity & Address (Passport, Voter ID, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, etc.) PAN Card Proof of each period of experience claimed, compiled in a single PDF in chronological order (offer letter, joining order, relieving letter, service certificate, relevant pay slips, etc.) PwBD Certificate as per the latest format (if applicable) Any other document supporting the candidature and claims made in the application Candidates called for document verification must additionally carry a printed, signed copy of the application form (with registration number) along with self-attested scanned copies of the same documents, plus one recent passport-size colour photograph, in the prescribed order.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Ensure you satisfy the necessary conditions and requirements before applying. Apply online through the registration format available in the Career Section of the RITES website, www.rites.com . Note down the "Registration No." generated on top of the online form for all further communication with RITES. Carefully fill in the "Identity Proof" details, and ensure the same document is available for production at later stages of selection. Upload all the required documents under the "Upload Document" section, ensuring scanned copies are clear and visible. Make the online payment under the "Make Payment" section, and download the final application form after successful payment. Keep a copy of the submitted application form and carry it at the time of selection, if called. Applications without successful fee payment will be treated as incomplete and summarily rejected. Candidates can apply for any number of vacancies separately, but only one application per vacancy is allowed, and an application once submitted cannot be altered.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application submission and online fee payment is 17 August 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 24 vacancies for Assistant Manager (Mechanical), including 1 post reserved for PwBD on a horizontal basis.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical)?
faq.answer: Candidates need a full-time Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related discipline, with a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience in production or mechanical maintenance.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 32 years as on 17 August 2026, with relaxations available for SC/ST, OBC-NCL, Ex-Servicemen, J&K Domicile, and PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹600 plus taxes for General/OBC candidates and ₹300 plus taxes for EWS/SC/ST/PwBD candidates, payable online only.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical)?
faq.answer: Selection involves a written test (125 questions, 2.5 hours, no negative marking), document scrutiny, and an interview, with 60% weightage to the written test and 40% to the interview.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical)?
faq.answer: The pay scale is ₹40,000–1,40,000, with pay fixed at the minimum of the scale, plus DA, HRA/lease accommodation, and other allowances as applicable.

faq.question: Q8. Is there negative marking in the RITES written test?
faq.answer: No, there is no negative marking in the RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) written test.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply for RITES Assistant Manager (Mechanical) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the Career Section of www.rites.com by registering, filling in details, uploading required documents, and making the online fee payment.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for RITES Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official website is www.rites.com, where the notification, online application, and all updates/corrigenda are published.
